aei.title: Anthropic Economic Index
aei.summary: Millions of anonymized Claude conversations mapped to O*NET tasks and occupations; open dataset. Early finding: ~36% of occupations used AI in ≥25% of tasks (Jan 2025), rising to ~49% by early 2026. Usage concentrated in software and technical writing; distinguishes automation vs. augmentation usage. The machine-readable base layer for this map.

aei-labor.title: Labor market impacts research
aei-labor.summary: Effective-coverage measure. No clear aggregate unemployment signal as of early 2026, but hiring of 22–25-year-old workers into the most-exposed roles slowed ~14% vs. counterfactual.

stanford-canaries.title: Canaries in the Coal Mine (Brynjolfsson, Chandar & Chen)
stanford-canaries.summary: ADP payroll microdata: early-career workers (22–25) in the most AI-exposed occupations show a relative employment decline of 13% in the original Aug 2025 paper, revised to ~16% in the Nov 2025 update — up to ~20% for young software developers. Declines concentrated in automative (not augmentative) AI applications; adjustment via employment, not wages. The single best early-warning empirical source; the live Canaries Dashboard updates with AEI data.

mit-iceberg.title: Iceberg Index
mit-iceberg.summary: Simulation: AI can technically perform the work of ~11.7% of the US labor market (~$1.2T in wages); visible tech-layoff disruption is only ~2.2% — the rest sits quietly in HR, logistics, and office administration.

challenger-2025.title: 2025 Year-End Job Cuts Report
challenger-2025.summary: Tracks announced US job cuts by stated reason: 54,836 cuts explicitly attributed to AI in 2025 (71,825 cumulative since tracking began in 2023). The live count of AI-attributed layoffs.

signalfire-2025.title: State of Talent Report 2025
signalfire-2025.summary: Big Tech new-grad hiring down more than 50% vs. 2019 (down 25% vs. 2023); startups down more than 30%; new grads now only ~7% of Big Tech hires. The entry-level-tech-hiring collapse in numbers.

eia-2026.title: US electricity demand outlook (Short-Term Energy Outlook)
eia-2026.summary: Strongest four-year growth in US electricity demand since 2000, fueled by datacenters — the demand signal behind the energy-buildout jobs branch. Goldman projects US datacenter power demand roughly doubling by 2027.

goldman-2023.title: The Potentially Large Effects of AI on Economic Growth (Hatzius et al.)
goldman-2023.summary: ~300M full-time-equivalent jobs exposed globally; ~25% of US work hours automatable. Office/admin support highest (46%), legal 44%, architecture/engineering 37%; physical and outdoor occupations least exposed. Exposure ≠ elimination: most affected workers see less than half their workload automated.

goldman-2026.title: An AI Job Apocalypse? (US Economics Analyst, June 25, 2026)
goldman-2026.summary: ~9% of the US workforce (~15M workers) displaced across a 10-year transition — up from the firm's earlier 6–7% estimate via a new methodology counting total worker flows out of existing jobs. Explicitly calls a "job apocalypse" unlikely: peak unemployment effect under one percentage point, with job creation in the power and datacenter buildout.

citi-2024.title: AI in Finance: Bot, Bank & Beyond (Citi GPS)
citi-2024.summary: 54% of banking jobs have high potential for automation — the highest share of any industry — plus a further 12% that could be augmented by AI.

imf-2024.title: Gen-AI: Artificial Intelligence and the Future of Work
imf-2024.summary: ~40% of global employment exposed; ~60% in advanced economies, of which roughly half may be harmed and half augmented. Inequality likely worsens. Its complementarity index by occupation is machine-readable and reusable.

wef-2025.title: Future of Jobs Report 2025
wef-2025.summary: 92M jobs displaced / 170M created by 2030 (net +78M, all drivers, not just AI). Fastest-declining: data entry clerks, bank tellers, cashiers, administrative assistants. ~40% of employers plan AI-related workforce reduction; 59 of 100 workers will need training by 2030.

amodei-axios.title: Dario Amodei interview on entry-level white-collar jobs
amodei-axios.summary: AI could eliminate ~50% of entry-level white-collar jobs within 1–5 years, with unemployment of 10–20%; names finance, law, consulting, and tech. "A duty to be honest about what is coming."

amodei-adolescence.title: The Adolescence of Technology
amodei-adolescence.summary: 20k-word essay doubling down: AI systems of beyond-Nobel capability possible by 2027; policy proposals including a token tax. Counterpoint noted in coverage: his 90%-of-code prediction was true inside Anthropic, 25–40% across the industry — the lab-view vs. diffusion-speed gap.

amodei-loving-grace.title: Machines of Loving Grace
amodei-loving-grace.summary: The optimistic branch: compressed decades of biology and health progress. Used here as the upside-scenario source.

aschenbrenner.title: Situational Awareness
aschenbrenner.summary: The influential fast-takeoff scenario document — AGI ~2027 via compute + algorithmic scaling, intelligence explosion, national-security framing. Used as the fast-timeline bound; not empirical.

kanungo-2026.title: Secondary synthesis: tracking Amodei's predictions vs. 2025–26 data (Kanungo)
kanungo-2026.summary: Entry-level tech hiring down 30–50% (SignalFire); 54,836 US layoffs explicitly AI-attributed in 2025 (Challenger); banks shrinking junior hiring classes by up to two-thirds; Anthropic engineers orchestrating fleets of Claudes. Verified against primaries 2026-07.

island-2025.title: Secondary synthesis: AI labor-market roundup
island-2025.summary: Secondary tracking of AI-attributed layoffs and hiring shifts through 2025. Used for color — numbers verified against primaries before quoting.

sq-2026.title: Secondary synthesis: occupation-level automation tracking
sq-2026.summary: Secondary synthesis of occupation-level automation data (paralegal document work, data entry) through 2026. Used for color — numbers verified against primaries before quoting.

framework.title: This project's mechanism framework
framework.summary: Scores marked "framework" rest primarily on the verification-speed / accountability-shield / demand-side logic described in the methodology, rather than a specific external study. Lowest evidential tier — flagged honestly via the per-job confidence field.
